body:
Markets closed Wednesday in mixed territory with total crypto market cap sliding 1.00% to $2.77T as traders took profits ahead of key employment data later this week. The session saw a clear divergence between large caps and smaller altcoins, with Bitcoin holding relatively well while Ethereum stumbled 2.36% in what appears to be continued rotation out of ETH-heavy positions. Volume remained robust at $111.1B, suggesting institutional activity rather than retail panic, though the Fear & Greed index barely budged from yesterday's 46 to today's 47, keeping sentiment firmly neutral.

Bitcoin printed $81,042, down just 0.84% on the day but maintaining its 6.60% weekly gain that has caught many bears off guard. The king coin is grinding higher in what looks like classic accumulation above the $80,000 psychological level, with $39.96B in daily volume indicating serious institutional interest. Key resistance sits at $82,500 where we've seen repeated rejections, while support has solidified around $79,500. Bitcoin dominance ticked up to 58.5%, reinforcing the flight to quality theme that's dominated May trading.

Ethereum's 2.36% drop to $2,331 stands out as the day's weakest major performer, with the selloff accelerating into the close on heavy $21.89B volume. This looks like more than just profit-taking, possibly reflecting concerns about upcoming regulatory clarity around ETH staking yields. Solana bucked the trend with a modest 0.88% gain to $89.39, while BNB added 0.86% to $649.4 as Binance's ecosystem continues attracting institutional flows. Chainlink deserves mention with its 9.91% weekly surge bringing LINK to $10.02, though it gave back 0.67% today as traders locked in gains.

The macro picture remains dominated by rate uncertainty as markets position for Friday's jobs report, with any surprise strength likely to pressure risk assets including crypto. Trump-linked American Bitcoin's $82M Q1 loss reported today highlights the mining sector's ongoing struggles with energy costs and AI competition, while BNY Mellon's crypto expansion in Abu Dhabi signals continued institutional infrastructure buildout. Regulatory winds appear favorable with multiple custody banks expanding services, though the Samourai Wallet legal battle reminds traders that enforcement risks persist.

Friday's employment data represents the week's biggest catalyst, with consensus expecting modest job growth that could keep Fed policy steady. Any material deviation moves Bitcoin either toward $85,000 on dovish data or back below $79,000 on hawkish surprises. Ethereum needs to reclaim $2,400 to halt the technical damage, while altcoin strength depends entirely on BTC clearing resistance cleanly. The setup favors range-bound action until employment clarity, but breakouts in either direction should move fast given current positioning.
